The antempasterior relation refers to the forward or backward positioning of the maxilla relative to the mandible. This relationship can be assessed using various craniofacial angles, but specifically for measuring this anterior-posterior alignment directly involves comparing a reference point on the upper jaw (maxilla) with that on the lower jaw (mandible).

Answer Choice: B
The correct option measures the direct vertical distance from the nasion (N-point) to the Frankfort horizontal plane through the condylar process which reflects accurate anterior-posterior position of the mandible relative to the skull base.

raw_response:
The antempasterior relation refers to the forward or backward positioning of the maxilla relative to the mandible. This relationship can be assessed using various craniofacial angles, but specifically for measuring this anterior-posterior alignment directly involves comparing a reference point on the upper jaw (maxilla) with that on the lower jaw (mandible).

Answer Choice: B
The correct option measures the direct vertical distance from the nasion (N-point) to the Frankfort horizontal plane through the condylar process which reflects accurate anterior-posterior position of the mandible relative to the skull base. 

So my final answer would be Option B - Angle between SN & FH Plane.
